items.title: Chief Future Officer: Kate Gulliver, Wayfair
items.description: This episode profiles Wayfair CFO Kate Gulliver, who is leading the home goods retailer's efforts to achieve profitable growth during a challenging period for the housing market -- as well as launchin

items.title: How Japan's Top Eyewear Maker is Passing Inflation Test
items.description: JINS rose to prominence 25 years ago with its bargain-basement ¥4,990 eyeglasses, becoming a household name for cheap chic as falling or stagnant prices took hold. Now, the Tokyo-based lensmaker is po

items.title: US Launches Strikes on Iran for Second Straight Day
items.description: The US military said it struck Iran for the second straight day, an escalation of violence that threatens to strain an already fragile ceasefire. Bloomberg's Jeff Mason has the latest developments. (S

items.title: BOK’s Shin Sees Need for Rate Hike Ahead of Next Week’s Meeting
items.description: Bank of Korea Governor Shin Hyun Song told parliament that policy rates will need to rise at an appropriate time, reinforcing the bank’s hawkish stance just a week before a widely expected rate hike.

items.title: NZ Manufacturing Hits 5-Year High as Breman Highlights Recovery
items.description: New Zealand manufacturing expanded at the fastest pace in almost five years in June as central bank Governor Anna Breman pointed to a burgeoning economic recovery, underscoring this week’s decision to

items.title: High Yields Set Stage for More Global Bond Inflow Into Indonesia
items.description: Global inflows into Indonesian sovereign bonds are poised to extend, with investors saying they see value in some of emerging Asia’s highest yields on bets that a bruising round of interest-rate hikes

items.title: Damaged Manhattan Tower Owner to Reconstruct 15 Floors After Evacuation
items.description: The developer behind the Midtown Manhattan tower that was evacuated over structural concerns Tuesday says it has a plan to fix the issue, including reconstructing 15 floors that were added to the orig

items.title: Apple Supplier Luxshare to Trade After HK’s Biggest 2026 Listing
items.description: Apple Inc. supplier Luxshare Precision Industry Co. is set to begin trading in Hong Kong on Thursday after raising HK$24.3 billion ($3.1 billion) in the city’s biggest listing this year so far.

items.title: Gold Holds Decline as US Strikes on Iran Magnify Inflation Risks
items.description: Gold held a decline as a second day of US strikes against Iran drove energy prices higher and intensified concerns around inflation.

items.title: Luxury Car Firm DuPont Registry Group to Pick Banks for IPO
items.description: DuPont Registry Group, a luxury car marketplace and media company, has picked banks for a US initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Samsung, SK Hynix and Leveraged ETFs Drive 70% of Korea Trading
items.description: Retail fervor over leveraged ETFs in South Korea has grown so intense that the products, together with the two chipmaker stocks they track, now make up more than 70% of trading value in the $4.3 trill

items.title: Noteholders to Aston Martin Sign Cooperation Pact Amid Debt Rout
items.description: A group of creditors to luxury car maker Aston Martin has signed a cooperation pact as earnings pressure weighs on the company’s debt.

items.title: Harley-Davidson’s Credit Ratings Get Cut to Junk By S&P
items.description: S&P Global Ratings cut Harley-Davidson Inc.’s credit grades to junk status on Wednesday, citing the company’s strategy to start selling lower-cost motorcycles to boost revenue.

items.title: SK Hynix US Offering Is More Than Seven Times Oversubscribed
items.description: SK Hynix Inc.’s US listing is more than seven times oversubscribed, according to people familiar with the matter, as the South Korean memory chipmaker prepares to price its offering Thursday.

items.title: Asian Shares Rise as Chips Rally, Brent Advances: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian shares climbed, led by chipmakers, as optimism over AI-driven demand rekindled appetite for semiconductor stocks. Oil extended gains after the US struck Iran for a second day.

items.title: Oil Extends Gain as Fresh US Strikes Against Iran Rattle Market
items.description: Oil extended a surge as the US struck targets in Iran for a second day, raising risks for energy supplies from the Middle East.

items.title: Trump Says Ukraine Can Make Patriot Missiles

items.title: Trader Joe's, Lowe's Embrace Mini Mania To Boost Sales
items.description: Mini mania is sweeping through US retailers as shoppers on a budget love colorful, charming, tiny versions of common products. Bloomberg’s Sunny Kim has more.  (Source: Bloomberg)
